The user wants me to remediate an OpenSCAP finding for installing the policycoreutils-python-utils package on Rocky Linux 10. Let me analyze the requirements:

1. I need to use ONLY the provided tools: run_cmd, read_file, write_file
2. Use run_cmd for EXACTLY ONE shell command at a time (no chaining)
3. Commands run as root (no sudo prefix)
4. Always use read_file to inspect before modifying
5. Do NOT append duplicate lines - use sed -i to modify existing values in-place
6. If a line is commented, uncomment it and set the value
7. Prefer minimal, reversible changes

The finding is about installing the policycoreutils-python-utils package. The remediation should:
- Check if the package is already installed
- If not installed, install it
- Include platform applicability check (rpm --quiet -q kernel-core) before attempting installation
- Include error handling for dnf install
